headlines.title: Protesters in Serbia doubt Vucic resignation will bring change
headlines.snippet: Thousands of protesters in Kraljevo, Serbia, are skeptical that Aleksandar Vucic's promised resignation will lead to meaningful change. They gathered to express their doubts following his pledge to step down.

headlines.title: Pope Leo prays for Venezuela’s recovery after deadly quakes
headlines.snippet: Pope Leo has extended his sympathies to the people of Venezuela following two severe earthquakes. These natural disasters resulted in the deaths of at least 1,400 individuals and caused widespread devastation.

headlines.title: Israel kills four Palestinians in Gaza as attacks on tents continue
headlines.snippet: Israeli forces have reportedly restricted Palestinian access to Al-Aqsa Mosque and prevented the call to prayer at the Ibrahimi Mosque. This comes amid reports of Israeli forces killing four Palestinians in Gaza.

headlines.title: Hope of finding more Venezuela quake survivors fades despite late rescues
headlines.snippet: Hope is dwindling for the discovery of additional survivors from the Venezuelan earthquakes, despite some successful late rescues. A 60-year-old woman was found after 86 hours, yet tens of thousands remain unaccounted for.

headlines.title: Gaza’s displaced families face worsening living conditions
headlines.snippet: Displaced families in Gaza are enduring increasingly dire living conditions. Rat infestations and overflowing sewage in tents are contributing to the spread of disease due to a lack of sanitation facilities.

headlines.title: Gaza journalists work from tents after Israel destroys media offices
headlines.snippet: Gaza's journalists are now working from makeshift tents after their media offices were destroyed by Israeli attacks. According to a report, a significant percentage of journalists have lost homes or been displaced, and many have been killed in the conflict.

headlines.title: Video: Israeli forces using a bulldozer to destroy Palestinian olive groves
headlines.snippet: Video footage shows Israeli forces using a bulldozer to clear Palestinian olive groves. The action appears to be part of broader military operations in the region.

headlines.title: US and Iran agree to resume Strait of Hormuz talks: Report
headlines.snippet: According to Axios, Iran and the United States have agreed to resume talks regarding the Strait of Hormuz. This agreement follows several days of escalating attacks and counterattacks, potentially halting further hostilities.

headlines.title: Israel shells Syrian village near occupied Golan Heights
headlines.snippet: Syria's official news agency SANA reports that Israeli forces have shelled a village in southern Syria, near the occupied Golan Heights. Israeli military aircraft were also observed flying over areas in Quneitra and Deraa.

headlines.title: ‘Financial death penalty’: How US sanctions are upending the lives of ICC judges
headlines.snippet: Three International Criminal Court (ICC) judges have reportedly had their financial lives severely impacted by sanctions imposed by the Trump administration. These sanctions include frozen bank accounts and loss of essential financial services.

headlines.title: Israel destroys Hezbollah underground infrastructure in southern Lebanon
headlines.snippet: Israel has announced the destruction of underground Hezbollah infrastructure located in southern Lebanon. This action is part of ongoing military operations in the region.

headlines.title: Iran canceled participation in technical talks over recent attacks, state TV reports
headlines.snippet: State TV reports that Iran has canceled its participation in technical talks. This decision follows recent attacks, the nature of which remains unspecified.

headlines.title: Turkey denounces Israel’s ‘political’ recognition of Armenia genocide
headlines.snippet: Turkey has voiced strong disapproval of Israel's 'political' recognition of the Armenian genocide. This statement comes amid ongoing tensions in the region.

headlines.title: Death toll from Venezuela quakes rises to 1,450
headlines.snippet: The confirmed death toll from the earthquakes in Venezuela has risen to 1,450. Rescue and recovery efforts are ongoing in the affected areas.

headlines.title: Qatar says citizen killed from shrapnel due to ‘military operations’
headlines.snippet: Qatar has reported that one of its citizens was killed by shrapnel resulting from 'military operations.' The specifics of these operations have not been detailed.

headlines.title: Pakistan says carried out operation, strikes along Afghan border
headlines.snippet: Pakistan's military stated that it conducted operations and airstrikes along the Afghan border, resulting in the deaths of 29 militants. These actions targeted known militant hideouts.

headlines.title: Iran warns ships not to bypass its chosen Hormuz route
headlines.snippet: Iran has issued a warning to ships against bypassing its designated route through the Strait of Hormuz. This warning comes amid heightened fears of renewed tensions in the Middle East following recent US-Iran attacks.

headlines.title: Kurds rally in Turkey's southeast for jailed PKK leader
headlines.snippet: Thousands of Kurds gathered in Diyarbakir, a city in southeastern Turkey. Their rally was in support of the release of jailed PKK leader Abdullah Ocalan.

headlines.title: Israeli strikes, shelling in Gaza kill at least 4 Palestinians
headlines.snippet: At least four Palestinians, including a 13-year-old girl, were killed in Israeli strikes and shelling across Gaza. Residents reported renewed shelling despite a reported ceasefire.

headlines.title: Israel recognises Armenian genocide in rebuke to Turkey
headlines.snippet: Israel's decision to recognize the events of 1915 as genocide has drawn criticism. This move is seen by some as ironic given the ongoing conflict in Gaza.

headlines.snippet: Foreign dignitaries are expected to begin arriving in Iran on Friday for the funeral ceremonies of the martyred Leader of the Islamic Revolution, Ayatollah Seyyed Ali Khamenei. This was announced by the First Vice President.

headlines.snippet: Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held separate meetings in Baghdad with Iraqi officials to coordinate funeral arrangements for the martyred Leader of the Islamic Revolution. Discussions also covered regional security matters.

headlines.snippet: Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that ending the war in Lebanon and ensuring its sovereignty are key components of the Islamabad memorandum of understanding between Iran and the United States.

headlines.snippet: Sardasht is still awaiting justice and truth regarding the chemical attack that occurred there. The situation remains unresolved after many years.

headlines.snippet: Flights between Tehran and Dubai are scheduled to resume tomorrow. This resumption is expected to facilitate travel and trade between the two cities.

headlines.snippet: The Leader has ordered the pursuit of alleged war crimes committed by the US and Israel against Iran. This directive aims to hold accountable those responsible.
